entries.regulation_name: Health Products (Advertisement of Therapeutic Products) Regulations
entries.citation: Health Products (Advertisement of Therapeutic Products) Regulations — Regulation 4
entries.summary: Therapeutic product ads must not claim to treat, prevent, or cure diseases unless HSA-approved indications are reflected.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Disease treatment, prevention, cure, or prescription-only medicine positioning. ACTION: REJECT — remove unapproved therapeutic claims. CHECK: Headlines, pack shots, testimonials, and disease names in copy. ESCALATE IF: Scheduled medicine, serious disease, or Rx-only product referenced.

entries.summary: Medical device advertising must stay within registered intended use and must not imply unregistered clinical outcomes.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Clinical outcome, diagnosis, or treatment claims for medical devices. ACTION: REJECT or REWRITE to registered intended use only. CHECK: Device class, UDI references, and clinical benefit statements. ESCALATE IF: Claim exceeds registered intended purpose or targets serious conditions.

entries.summary: Health supplement ads may only use permitted general health claims aligned with product classification and substantiation.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Nutritional, wellness, or general health benefit claims for supplements. ACTION: WARN — require substantiation or qualify wording. CHECK: Ingredient lists, dosage claims, and before/after wellness outcomes. ESCALATE IF: Claim implies disease modification or medicinal effect.

entries.summary: Traditional medicine advertising is limited to culturally accepted health maintenance claims without modern disease-treatment wording.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: TCM, traditional remedy, or herbal product health benefit claims. ACTION: REWRITE — use permitted traditional health maintenance language. CHECK: Herb names, efficacy timelines, and disease references. ESCALATE IF: Modern biomedical cure or anti-disease language appears.

entries.citation: Singapore Code of Advertising Practice — Rule 3 (Comparisons)
entries.summary: Comparative and superiority claims must be fair, identifiable, and substantiated before publication.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Versus, better-than, leading, or ranked superiority claims. ACTION: WARN — require substantiation file or soften claim. CHECK: Named competitors, test methods, and sample sizes cited. ESCALATE IF: Unidentifiable competitor or unsubstantiated head-to-head data.

entries.citation: Singapore Code of Advertising Practice — Rule 2.4 (Influencer content)
entries.summary: Paid influencer and branded content must be clearly disclosed as advertising across all formats.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Influencer posts, gifted product reviews, or affiliate promotions. ACTION: REWRITE — add clear sponsorship disclosure. CHECK: Hashtags, captions, video overlays, and platform-native ad labels. ESCALATE IF: Covert promotion without #ad, Paid Partnership, or equivalent.

entries.regulation_name: Health Products (Advertisement of Health Products) Regulations
entries.citation: Health Products (Advertisement of Health Products) Regulations — Regulation 6
entries.summary: Quantified efficacy, speed, or results claims for health products require valid substantiation and must not be absolute.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Percentage improvement, time-bound results, or guaranteed outcomes. ACTION: WARN — require test evidence or qualify with typical results. CHECK: Study citations, sample size, and measurement methodology. ESCALATE IF: 100% efficacy, miracle results, or unsupported statistics.

entries.citation: Health Products Act — Section 12 (Misleading use of certification)
entries.summary: Ads must not misuse HSA registration, GMP, or quality marks to imply unapproved benefits.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: HSA logo, GMP badge, ISO mark, or quality seal in creative. ACTION: WARN — verify mark scope matches claim. CHECK: Registration number, certificate scope, and expiry. ESCALATE IF: Mark implies therapeutic approval beyond registration class.

entries.citation: Singapore Code of Advertising Practice — Rule 5 (Testimonials and awards)
entries.summary: Awards, seals, and testimonials must be genuine, current, and representative; fabricated endorsements are prohibited.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Award badges, expert endorsements, or consumer testimonial quotes. ACTION: WARN — verify authenticity and typicality. CHECK: Award issuer, date, and whether testimonial is paid. ESCALATE IF: Fabricated review, outdated award, or atypical result presented as norm.

entries.summary: Green, recyclable, and carbon-neutral claims must be specific, substantiated, and not overstate environmental benefit.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Eco-friendly, sustainable, carbon-neutral, or recyclable claims. ACTION: WARN — require lifecycle evidence or narrow claim scope. CHECK: Certification body, scope boundary, and disposal instructions. ESCALATE IF: Vague greenwash without measurable basis.

entries.summary: Synthetic or AI-generated people, scenes, or voice in ads should be disclosed where consumers could be misled.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: AI-generated models, deepfake-style imagery, or synthetic voiceover. ACTION: REWRITE — add AI/synthetic media disclosure. CHECK: Visual realism, likeness rights, and disclosure placement. ESCALATE IF: Synthetic human likeness mimicking real person without consent.

entries.citation: Singapore Code of Advertising Practice — Rule 4.2 (Before/after imagery)
entries.summary: Before/after and transformation images must reflect typical results and must not be misleading through staging or editing.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Before/after photos, split-screen transformations, or retouched results. ACTION: WARN — require typicality disclaimer or substantiation. CHECK: Lighting, makeup, time interval, and digital editing. ESCALATE IF: Dramatic atypical result shown without qualification.

entries.summary: Safety claims such as non-toxic, child-safe, or side-effect-free must not contradict known product risks or label warnings.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Non-toxic, side-effect-free, child-safe, or zero-risk safety claims. ACTION: REJECT — align with label warnings and known risks. CHECK: Contraindications, age restrictions, and hazard pictograms. ESCALATE IF: Claim contradicts HSA label or pharmacovigilance data.

entries.summary: Medicine ads must not promote unregistered products or make prohibited therapeutic claims without NPRA approval.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Cure, treat, prevent disease, or Rx medicine promotion in consumer ads. ACTION: REJECT — remove prohibited medicinal claims. CHECK: MAL registration number, product category, and indication wording. ESCALATE IF: Unregistered product or serious disease claim without approval.

entries.summary: Medical device advertising must not claim unregistered intended purposes or clinical outcomes beyond approval.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Clinical benefit or diagnostic claims for medical devices. ACTION: REWRITE — limit to registered intended use. CHECK: MDA registration status and device class. ESCALATE IF: Unregistered device or expanded clinical claim.

entries.regulation_name: Guidelines on Advertisement of Health Supplements
entries.citation: Guidelines on Advertisement of Health Supplements — Clause 5
entries.summary: Health supplement ads are restricted to general health maintenance claims and must not imply medicinal effects.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Vitamin, herbal, or nutritional health benefit claims. ACTION: WARN — verify against permitted claim list. CHECK: Product notification status and ingredient claims. ESCALATE IF: Disease treatment or pharmacological effect implied.

entries.regulation_name: Control of Drugs and Cosmetics Regulations
entries.citation: Control of Drugs and Cosmetics Regulations — Regulation 18A
entries.summary: Cosmetic ads may describe appearance effects but must not claim therapeutic or disease-related benefits.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Skin healing, anti-disease, or medicinal cosmetic claims. ACTION: REJECT — reclassify as cosmetic appearance claim only. CHECK: Product notification (NOT) number and claim type. ESCALATE IF: Therapeutic or NPRA drug-like claim on cosmetic.

entries.citation: Malaysian Code of Advertising Practice — Appendix C (Comparisons)
entries.summary: Comparative claims must be truthful, verifiable, and not denigrate competitors unfairly.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Brand versus brand, market leader, or superiority comparisons. ACTION: WARN — require substantiation on file. CHECK: Named competitor, test protocol, and date of comparison. ESCALATE IF: False denigration or unverifiable head-to-head claim.

entries.citation: Malaysian Code of Advertising Practice — Rule 2 (Identification)
entries.summary: Advertorial and sponsored social content must be clearly identified as paid advertising.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: KOL posts, paid reviews, or branded editorial content. ACTION: REWRITE — add sponsorship identification. CHECK: Platform ad tools, #ad labels, and Bahasa/English disclosure. ESCALATE IF: Undisclosed paid promotion presented as organic opinion.

entries.summary: Performance and results claims for consumer goods must be accurate and supported by adequate testing.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Speed, durability, energy saving, or efficacy statistics. ACTION: WARN — require test report or qualify claim. CHECK: Test standard, sample size, and measurement conditions. ESCALATE IF: Absolute or exaggerated performance without basis.

entries.citation: Consumer Protection Act 1999 — Part III (Misleading conduct)
entries.summary: Misleading or deceptive conduct in trade including false product representations is prohibited.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: False origin, fake discounts, or deceptive product descriptions. ACTION: REJECT — correct material misrepresentation. CHECK: Price history, stock availability, and product specs. ESCALATE IF: Systematic consumer deception or regulatory notice received.

entries.citation: Price Control and Anti-Profiteering Act 2011 — Section 10
entries.summary: Promotional pricing must reflect genuine savings; strikethrough and limited-time offers must not mislead consumers.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Was/now pricing, percentage off, or limited-time discount claims. ACTION: WARN — verify price history and offer terms. CHECK: Reference price period, currency, and exclusions. ESCALATE IF: Inflated reference price or fake urgency.

entries.regulation_name: Consumer Protection (Certification and Standards Marks) Regulations 2010
entries.citation: Consumer Protection (Certification and Standards Marks) Regulations 2010 — Regulation 5
entries.summary: SIRIM, MS, and other certification marks may only be used for certified product scopes and valid periods.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: SIRIM, MS ISO, or halal/certification logo in ad creative. ACTION: WARN — verify certificate scope and validity. CHECK: Certificate number, product SKU scope, and expiry. ESCALATE IF: Expired certification or mark used beyond scope.

entries.summary: Drug advertising to the public is restricted; prohibited claims include cure guarantees and unapproved indications.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Drug cure, treatment guarantee, or OTC drug promotion with Rx-only claims. ACTION: REJECT — remove prohibited drug advertising. CHECK: Thai FDA registration, ad approval status, and indication text. ESCALATE IF: Unapproved drug ad or serious disease cure claim.

entries.summary: Medical device ads must align with Thai FDA registered intended use and approved advertising content.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Clinical outcome or diagnostic claims for medical devices. ACTION: REWRITE — match registered intended use only. CHECK: TFDA license number and device classification. ESCALATE IF: Unregistered device or off-label clinical claim.

entries.citation: Notification of Food Supplements — Announcement Clause 4
entries.summary: Food supplement ads must use permitted health claims and must not imply medicinal or disease-treatment effects.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Vitamin, herbal, or functional food health benefit claims. ACTION: WARN — check against permitted claim list. CHECK: FDA notification number and Thai label claims. ESCALATE IF: Disease prevention or treatment language used.

entries.summary: Cosmetic advertising may claim appearance benefits but must not assert therapeutic or drug-like effects.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Skin disease treatment, healing, or medicinal cosmetic claims. ACTION: REJECT — limit to cosmetic appearance effects. CHECK: Cosmetic notification and ingredient restrictions. ESCALATE IF: Drug-like efficacy claim on cosmetic product.

entries.citation: Consumer Protection Act B.E. 2522 — Section 42 (Comparative advertising)
entries.summary: Comparative advertising must be truthful, not misleading, and must not unfairly disparage competitors.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Competitor comparison, market share, or superiority claims. ACTION: WARN — require objective substantiation. CHECK: Identifiable competitor, test date, and methodology. ESCALATE IF: False comparison or unfair denigration.

entries.citation: Guidelines on Identification of Advertisements — Article 3
entries.summary: Sponsored online and social media content must be clearly labeled as advertising to consumers.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Influencer, live commerce, or paid social posts. ACTION: REWRITE — add clear ad identification in Thai. CHECK: Caption labels, on-screen text, and platform ad tags. ESCALATE IF: Covert sponsorship without ad marker.

entries.summary: Product performance claims must not deceive consumers about quality, durability, or efficacy.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Durability, efficiency, or results statistics for consumer products. ACTION: WARN — require supporting test data. CHECK: TIS standard references and test conditions. ESCALATE IF: Exaggerated or unverifiable performance claim.

entries.summary: Unfair trade practices including false descriptions of goods or services are prohibited.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: False product origin, quality, or benefit descriptions. ACTION: REJECT — correct misleading representation. CHECK: Material terms, warranties, and conditions. ESCALATE IF: Pattern of deceptive trade practice.

entries.summary: Promotional and discount pricing must reflect genuine price reductions without inflated reference prices.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Discount percentage, flash sale, or strikethrough pricing. ACTION: WARN — verify prior selling price. CHECK: Reference price period and promotion duration. ESCALATE IF: Fake discount from inflated reference price.

entries.citation: TIS Product Certification Mark Requirements — Section 5
entries.summary: TIS and other standard marks may only appear on products and ads for valid certified models.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: TIS mark, Q mark, or standards logo in advertising. ACTION: WARN — verify certification covers advertised SKU. CHECK: Certificate ID, product model, and validity. ESCALATE IF: Mark used on non-certified variant.

entries.regulation_name: Government Regulation on Drug and Food Control (Advertising)
entries.summary: Drug advertising to consumers is heavily restricted; cure and unapproved therapeutic claims are prohibited.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Drug cure, Rx promotion, or disease treatment guarantees. ACTION: REJECT — remove prohibited drug claims. CHECK: BPOM registration (NA/ML), ad approval, and indication. ESCALATE IF: Unregistered drug or public Rx ad without approval.

entries.citation: BPOM Regulation on Medical Device Advertising — Article 8
entries.summary: Medical device ads must stay within BPOM-registered intended use and approved promotional materials.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Clinical outcome or expanded indication claims for devices. ACTION: REWRITE — align with registered intended use. CHECK: Device registration number and risk class. ESCALATE IF: Off-label or unregistered device promotion.

entries.regulation_name: BPOM Regulation on Health Supplement Claims
entries.citation: BPOM Regulation on Health Supplement Claims — Article 12
entries.summary: Health supplement ads must use BPOM-permitted claims and must not imply disease treatment or prevention.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Nutraceutical, herbal, or vitamin health benefit claims. ACTION: WARN — verify against BPOM permitted claim list. CHECK: ML registration, label claims, and dosage. ESCALATE IF: Disease treatment or medicinal effect implied.

entries.summary: Cosmetic advertising is limited to appearance and hygiene benefits without therapeutic or drug claims.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Healing, anti-disease, or medicinal skin claims on cosmetics. ACTION: REJECT — restrict to cosmetic benefit language. CHECK: Cosmetic notification and prohibited ingredients. ESCALATE IF: Drug-like therapeutic claim on cosmetic.

entries.summary: Comparative advertising must be honest, substantiated, and must not unfairly harm competitor reputation.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Brand comparison, best-in-class, or superiority claims. ACTION: WARN — require substantiation documentation. CHECK: Named competitor, evidence date, and claim scope. ESCALATE IF: Unsubstantiated superiority or unfair disparagement.

entries.summary: Advertisements including digital and influencer content must be clearly identifiable as commercial messages.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Sponsored posts, endorsements, or native advertising. ACTION: REWRITE — add clear ad identification. CHECK: Bahasa Indonesia disclosure, #iklan, and platform labels. ESCALATE IF: Hidden commercial content without identification.

entries.citation: Law No. 8/1999 on Consumer Protection — Article 8
entries.summary: Product efficacy and performance claims must be truthful and must not mislead about quality or results.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Efficacy percentages, guaranteed results, or performance stats. ACTION: WARN — require substantiation or qualify claim. CHECK: Test method, sample, and applicable product variant. ESCALATE IF: Absolute guarantee or fabricated test results.

entries.citation: Law No. 8/1999 on Consumer Protection — Article 18
entries.summary: Business actors must not provide false or misleading information about goods or services.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: False specs, deceptive offers, or misrepresented product benefits. ACTION: REJECT — correct material misrepresentation. CHECK: Offer terms, warranties, and product attributes. ESCALATE IF: Widespread consumer harm or regulatory inquiry.

entries.citation: Law No. 5/1999 on Prohibition of Monopolistic Practices — Article 15
entries.summary: Promotional pricing and discount claims must not deceive consumers about actual savings or price history.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Diskon, promo, or strikethrough price claims. ACTION: WARN — verify reference price and promotion terms. CHECK: Prior selling price period and exclusions. ESCALATE IF: Misleading discount from inflated baseline price.

entries.summary: SNI and halal certification marks may only be used where valid certification covers the advertised product.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: SNI logo, halal mark, or quality certification in ads. ACTION: WARN — verify certificate applies to SKU. CHECK: Certificate number, scope, and validity date. ESCALATE IF: Expired or scope-mismatched certification mark.

entries.summary: Prescription drug advertising to the general public is prohibited; OTC drug ads must comply with approved labeling.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Rx drug promotion, cure claims, or unapproved drug indications. ACTION: REJECT — remove prohibited pharmaceutical advertising. CHECK: YJ code, OTC classification, and approved labeling text. ESCALATE IF: Rx ad to public or unapproved efficacy claim.

entries.summary: Medical device advertising must reflect approved indications and must not expand beyond PMDA clearance.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Clinical outcome or unapproved indication for medical devices. ACTION: REWRITE — limit to approved intended use. CHECK: Approval number, risk class, and labeling. ESCALATE IF: Off-label or unapproved device promotion.

entries.regulation_name: Health Promotion Act (Foods with Function Claims)
entries.citation: Health Promotion Act — FFC Notification Requirements
entries.summary: Foods with Function Claims must use only notified claim wording; other health foods must avoid disease-related claims.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: FFC, nutraceutical, or general food health benefit claims. ACTION: WARN — match notified claim text exactly. CHECK: Consumer Affairs Agency notification number and claim scope. ESCALATE IF: Disease treatment or unnotified function claim.

entries.summary: Cosmetics and quasi-drugs may claim approved cosmetic effects but must not make unapproved medicinal claims.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Skin disease treatment or medicinal effect on cosmetics. ACTION: REJECT — use approved cosmetic effect language only. CHECK: Product category (cosmetic vs quasi-drug) and approved claims. ESCALATE IF: Medicinal claim on standard cosmetic product.

entries.regulation_name: Act against Unjustifiable Premiums and Misleading Representations
entries.citation: Act against Unjustifiable Premiums — Section 5 (Comparative representations)
entries.summary: Comparative and superiority representations must be substantiated and must not mislead consumers.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: No.1, best, versus competitor, or superiority claims. ACTION: WARN — require objective evidence. CHECK: Survey methodology, sample, and comparison date. ESCALATE IF: Unsubstantiated No.1 or misleading comparison.

entries.summary: Stealth marketing and undisclosed paid endorsements are prohibited; sponsored content must be clearly identified.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Influencer posts, paid reviews, or undisclosed brand partnerships. ACTION: REWRITE — add clear paid promotion disclosure. CHECK: Japanese disclosure labels, #PR, and platform ad settings. ESCALATE IF: Covert paid promotion presented as organic content.

entries.regulation_name: Act against Unjustifiable Premiums and Misleading Representations
entries.summary: Performance and efficacy representations must be based on fact and must not exaggerate beyond substantiation.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Performance stats, energy savings, or guaranteed results. ACTION: WARN — require test evidence or qualify wording. CHECK: Test standard (JIS), conditions, and typical results. ESCALATE IF: Exaggerated or absolute performance without basis.

entries.summary: Misleading representations about goods or services that affect consumer decisions are restricted.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: False quality, origin, or benefit representations. ACTION: REJECT — correct misleading material claims. CHECK: Contract terms, product specs, and offer conditions. ESCALATE IF: Systemic misleading practice or JFTC inquiry risk.

entries.regulation_name: Act against Unjustifiable Premiums and Misleading Representations
entries.summary: Double pricing and misleading discount representations are prohibited under fair representation rules.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Two-price display, percentage off, or limited-time sale claims. ACTION: WARN — verify reference price compliance. CHECK: Reference price period and actual selling history. ESCALATE IF: Fake reference price or misleading two-price tactic.

entries.summary: JIS and other certification marks may only be displayed for products within valid certified scope.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: JIS mark, JQA logo, or quality certification in ads. ACTION: WARN — verify certification covers product model. CHECK: Certificate number, scope, and validity. ESCALATE IF: Mark on non-certified product or expired cert.

entries.summary: Drug advertising is restricted; Rx drugs must not be promoted to consumers and claims must match MFDS approval.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Drug cure, Rx promotion, or unapproved drug efficacy claims. ACTION: REJECT — remove prohibited pharmaceutical ads. CHECK: MFDS approval, OTC/Rx status, and permitted labeling. ESCALATE IF: Public Rx ad or unapproved indication claim.

entries.summary: Medical device advertising must align with MFDS-permitted intended use and approved promotional content.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Clinical benefit or expanded indication for devices. ACTION: REWRITE — stay within approved intended use. CHECK: MFDS license, device class, and ad review status. ESCALATE IF: Off-label or unapproved device advertising.

entries.summary: Health functional foods may only use MFDS-approved function claims; disease treatment claims are prohibited.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: HFF, vitamin, or functional food health benefit claims. ACTION: WARN — match approved function claim text. CHECK: MFDS approval number and permitted claim wording. ESCALATE IF: Disease treatment or unapproved function claim.

entries.summary: Cosmetic ads may describe appearance effects but must not claim disease treatment or drug-like efficacy.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Skin disease, healing, or medicinal cosmetic claims. ACTION: REJECT — limit to permitted cosmetic effect claims. CHECK: Cosmetic report number and functional cosmetic status. ESCALATE IF: Drug-like therapeutic claim on cosmetic.

entries.regulation_name: Monopoly Regulation and Fair Trade Act
entries.citation: Monopoly Regulation and Fair Trade Act — Article 23
entries.summary: Comparative advertising must be objective, substantiated, and must not unfairly disparage competitors.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Versus competitor, market leader, or superiority claims. ACTION: WARN — require substantiation on file. CHECK: Identifiable competitor, evidence date, and methodology. ESCALATE IF: False or unfair comparative claim.

entries.citation: Guidelines on Labeling and Advertising — Article 5
entries.summary: Advertisements including influencer and native content must be clearly distinguishable as commercial messages.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Paid influencer, review, or native ad content. ACTION: REWRITE — add clear ad/sponsorship disclosure. CHECK: Korean disclosure text, #광고, and platform ad labels. ESCALATE IF: Undisclosed paid promotion.

entries.regulation_name: Act on Fair Labeling and Advertising
entries.citation: Act on Fair Labeling and Advertising — Article 3
entries.summary: Performance and efficacy claims must be truthful and supported by adequate evidence under fair labeling rules.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Performance statistics, guaranteed results, or efficacy percentages. ACTION: WARN — require substantiation or qualify claim. CHECK: Test method, KS standard, and typical results. ESCALATE IF: Absolute or unsubstantiated performance claim.

entries.regulation_name: Act on Fair Labeling and Advertising
entries.citation: Act on Fair Labeling and Advertising — Article 7
entries.summary: False or exaggerated labeling and advertising that deceives consumers is prohibited.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: False product description, deceptive offer, or misrepresented benefit. ACTION: REJECT — correct misleading representation. CHECK: Material terms, specs, and offer conditions. ESCALATE IF: Repeat violation or KFTC investigation risk.

entries.regulation_name: Act on Fair Labeling and Advertising (Pricing)
entries.citation: Act on Fair Labeling and Advertising — Article 4
entries.summary: Misleading price comparisons and false discount representations are restricted under fair advertising rules.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Discount rate, sale price, or reference price claims. ACTION: WARN — verify prior price and promotion terms. CHECK: Reference price period and exclusions. ESCALATE IF: Inflated reference price or fake discount.

entries.regulation_name: KS Mark and Quality Certification Requirements
entries.summary: KS and KC certification marks may only be used on products within valid certified scope and period.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: KS, KC, or quality certification logo in advertising. ACTION: WARN — verify certification covers advertised model. CHECK: Certificate ID, product scope, and expiry. ESCALATE IF: Expired or scope-mismatched certification mark.

entries.summary: Therapeutic goods advertising must comply with the Therapeutic Goods Advertising Code; prohibited claims include unapproved indications.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Disease treatment, cure, or unapproved therapeutic claims. ACTION: REJECT — remove non-compliant therapeutic advertising. CHECK: AUST R/L number, permitted indications, and required statements. ESCALATE IF: Serious disease claim or unapproved indication.

entries.summary: Medical device advertising must not claim benefits beyond ARTG inclusion and intended purpose.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Clinical outcome or unapproved indication for devices. ACTION: REWRITE — align with ARTG intended purpose. CHECK: ARTG number, device class, and instructions for use. ESCALATE IF: Off-label or unregistered device claim.

entries.summary: Complementary medicine ads must use permitted indications and mandatory warnings; disease claims require TGA approval.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Vitamin, herbal, or complementary health benefit claims. ACTION: WARN — verify permitted indication and warnings. CHECK: AUST L number, indication list, and mandatory statements. ESCALATE IF: Unapproved health claim or missing mandatory warning.

entries.summary: Products making therapeutic skin claims may be regulated as therapeutic goods; cosmetic ads must not cross into therapeutic claims.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Skin disease treatment or medicinal effect on skincare ads. ACTION: REJECT or ESCALATE — determine TGA vs cosmetic classification. CHECK: Primary purpose, ingredients, and claim wording. ESCALATE IF: Therapeutic claim on product not on ARTG.

entries.citation: Competition and Consumer Act 2010 — Schedule 2, s 29 (Comparative claims)
entries.summary: Comparative claims must be accurate and substantiated; false comparisons may breach the Australian Consumer Law.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Versus, better-than, or market leadership comparisons. ACTION: WARN — require substantiation before publication. CHECK: Named competitor, test method, and comparison date. ESCALATE IF: False or misleading comparative claim.

entries.summary: Influencer and native advertising must be clearly identifiable as marketing communication.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Influencer, gifted, or affiliate promotional content. ACTION: REWRITE — add clear ad identification (#ad, Ad, Paid). CHECK: Caption, overlay, and platform-native ad disclosure. ESCALATE IF: Undisclosed paid promotion.

entries.summary: Performance representations must not mislead; businesses must have reasonable grounds for quantified claims.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Performance stats, savings claims, or guaranteed outcomes. ACTION: WARN — require reasonable substantiation. CHECK: Test standard (AS/NZS), conditions, and typical results. ESCALATE IF: Misleading or unsubstantiated performance claim.

entries.summary: Misleading or deceptive conduct in trade or commerce is prohibited under the Australian Consumer Law.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: False product claims, bait advertising, or deceptive offers. ACTION: REJECT — correct misleading conduct. CHECK: Material terms, pricing, and product attributes. ESCALATE IF: Systemic ACL breach or ACCC investigation risk.

entries.regulation_name: Australian Consumer Law (Certification and approval claims)
entries.citation: Australian Consumer Law — Schedule 2, s 29 (False certifications)
entries.summary: False or misleading representations about approvals, certifications, or quality marks are prohibited.
entries.review_guidance: TRIGGER: Certification logo, award, or approval badge in creative. ACTION: WARN — verify certification validity and scope. CHECK: Certifying body, product scope, and expiry. ESCALATE IF: False certification or mark beyond scope.
